steps.title: Factor out x from the left side
steps.content:
Rewrite the left side by factoring out the variable:
$$-6x + 42px = (-6 + 42p)x$$
So the equation becomes:
$$(-6 + 42p)x = 84$$

steps.title: Recognize when a linear equation has no solution
steps.content:
A linear equation $Ax = B$ has no solution only when the coefficient $A = 0$ but the constant $B \neq 0$, resulting in the false statement $0 = B$.

For this equation, we need the coefficient of $x$ to equal zero:
$$-6 + 42p = 0$$

steps.title: Solve for p
steps.content:
Solve for $p$:
$$42p = 6$$
$$p = \frac{6}{42} = \frac{1}{7}$$
Verify: when $p = \frac{1}{7}$, the coefficient $-6 + 42(\frac{1}{7}) = -6 + 6 = 0$, giving us $0 = 84$—a false statement with no solution. That matches choice D — D is correct.
